title: Radiometric Sensor Calibration
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Radiometric Sensor Calibration: Absolute radiometric calibration: determine gain and offset coefficients converting DN to physical radiance/reflectance. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.calibration.vicarious…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~20; sensor_degradation_drift_percent_yr, BRDF_angular_effect set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: TOA_reflectance = gain * DN + offset; atmospheric correction: rho_surface = (rho_TOA - rho_path) / T_atm
E.world_state_x: calibration coefficients (gain, offset) per spectral band for satellite sensor
E.observation_y: DN values for calibration targets (pseudo-invariant sites, vicarious sites, on-board lamps)

W.regime: Existence of the recovered calibration_coefficient_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 20); sensor_degradation_drift_percent_yr dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: radiometric uncertainty < 3% for CEOS-calibrated sensors. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~20. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |sensor_degradation_drift_percent_yr|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_cal_sites * N_bands) per calibration update per iteration
